script_text: Little pieces stick on the front and back of words.
The front ones are prefixes. The back ones are suffixes.
Each one changes the word's meaning!
UN means not. Unhappy means not happy.
RE means again. Redo means do again.
ER means one who does. Teacher means one who teaches.
LY turns a word into how. Quickly means in a quick way.
When you meet a long word, peel off the prefix and suffix. Find the root!
Now you are ready to read prefixes and suffixes!
